summary: Attempted to decompile the hammer item by adding src/melee/it/items/ithammer.c and ithammer.h, moving ithammer from asm object linkage to C object linkage in obj_files.mk. The PR was closed unmerged in favor of a later updated PR that also matched data. Discussion indicates the file progressed from an estimated ~79% match to being very close or possibly fully matching under the newer 1.2.5n compiler, with remaining concerns described as register swaps or mis-scheduled epilogues rather than functional logic.
what_changed: Added a new C implementation file for the hammer item at src/melee/it/items/ithammer.c with many it_80293xxx through it_802943xx functions.

Added src/melee/it/items/ithammer.h containing the hammer header guard, type include, prototypes for local hammer routines, and temporary extern declarations for item helper functions used by the C file.

Updated obj_files.mk to remove $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/it/items/ithammer.s.o from TEXT_O_FILES and add $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/ithammer.c.o, switching the hammer item from assembly to C compilation.
Implemented hammer behavior around item state/animation transitions using Item_80268E5C, it_8026B390, it_8026D62C, it_8026E15C, it_8026E8C4, it_80272860, it_8026DF34, and related item helpers.
Added logic using GET_ITEM, Item, Item_GObj, HSD_GObj, Article, ItemCommonData, ItemAttr, Vec3, lb_8000B1CC, lbVector_Normalize, HSD_Randf, and HSD_Randi.
Introduced a small static inline helper named thing that checks item->xD4C and calls it_80272A3C on the hammer JObj returned by it_80272CC0(arg0, 3) when the counter is non-positive.
Some non-matching or problematic source lines were left commented in the diff excerpt, especially around converting result.x sign into a direction scalar and absolute x component.
smart_moves: Kept the assembly-to-C migration explicit in obj_files.mk by removing the asm object and adding the C object in the same PR, making the build-system effect easy to audit.
Used existing project item types and macros such as GET_ITEM, Item_GObj, HSD_GObj, ItemAttr, Article, and Vec3 rather than inventing independent hammer-only data structures.
Factored repeated xD4C/JObj behavior into a local inline helper, reducing repeated code in many state transition functions.
Preserved commented alternatives for a known matching-sensitive sign/absolute-value expression, which documents where codegen was being tuned.
Tracked remaining mismatch with decomp.me scratches and discussion, narrowing the issue from broad incompleteness to register-swap or epilogue scheduling concerns.
decomp_lessons: For item actor files, object-list migration is part of the decomp change: remove the asm .s.o and add the src .c.o only when the C file is intended to replace that assembly unit.
Header work during early item decomp may require temporary extern declarations for nearby item helper functions, but these should be revisited later for proper shared headers once symbols stabilize.
State machine item code often matches through small wrapper functions that only call Item_80268E5C with state IDs and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, then perform shared cleanup or transition helpers.

Matching conditionals that convert vector sign into either -1 or 1 can be sensitive; equivalent ternary expressions, explicit if/else blocks, and absolute-value rewrites may produce different register allocation or scheduling.

Compiler version matters: reviewers noted apparent mis-scheduled epilogues that might match with the newer 1.2.5n compiler, so do not overfit source to old compiler artifacts before checking the current compiler.
A PR can be useful decomp evidence even if closed unmerged, especially when superseded by a later PR; preserve scratch links and mismatch notes for follow-up.
naming_conventions: New item files followed the existing path/name convention src/melee/it/items/ithammer.c and src/melee/it/items/ithammer.h.
The header guard used the project-style uppercase path-derived name MELEE_IT_ITEMS_ITHAMMER_H.
Most functions remained address-based names such as it_80293D94, it_8029402C, and it_80294344, indicating behavior names had not yet been recovered.
Temporary variables in the submitted code often used decompiler-style names such as temp_r31, temp_r4, temp_r3, and result.
The local helper name thing is non-semantic and should not be reused as a naming model; it reflects early matching work rather than final documentation quality.
The header comments marked some prototypes as extern or static, but the evidence suggests these were rough annotations rather than polished API documentation.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used decomp.me scratches for iterative matching: initial source link nF6aS, later very-close scratch Q1rJA, and a compiler-related scratch Xaf1U.
Known problematic/non-matching lines were commented out while a matching-sensitive replacement was tested, especially around result.x sign handling.
The author reported the remaining issue was down to a register swap; a reviewer later observed no obvious regswaps but did see mis-scheduled epilogues.
Reviewer feedback suggested testing with the newer 1.2.5n compiler because epilogue scheduling differences might disappear there.

The PR title reported ~79% match, but later comments imply the source may have become much closer, possibly whole-file matching under updated compiler assumptions; final merge evidence is absent because the PR was superseded.

review_feedback.author: RevoSucks
review_feedback.feedback: Asked for an update on the PR status.

review_feedback.author: JPeisach
review_feedback.feedback: Reported that the remaining mismatch was down to a register swap and later shared a very-close decomp.me scratch.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Said they did not see register swaps in the scratch, but saw mis-scheduled epilogues likely to match with the new 1.2.5n compiler; if the scratch was the whole file, they believed it might be a 100% match.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Responded to the 1.2.5n compiler point with another decomp.me scratch link.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Closed the PR in favor of an updated PR that also matched data.

searchable_terms: closed in favor of updated PR that also matches data
follow_up_queries: Find the updated PR that superseded PR #941 and matched hammer item data.
Check whether ithammer.c later gained named functions or cleaned-up header prototypes.
Compare PR #941 ithammer.c against the merged hammer implementation to identify which matching tactics survived.
Search for project-wide migration to compiler 1.2.5n and its effect on epilogue scheduling mismatches.
Audit temporary extern declarations in ithammer.h against current shared item headers.
